user_query:
You're being brought in to design a secure authentication and authorization layer for our TownSquare API. Please read the following inputs under input/ and then produce four concrete deliverables under output/.

Inputs to read:
- input/requirements.md — high-level product/security requirements for TownSquare
- input/policies.yaml — draft roles/permissions and policy notes from our team
- input/users.json — sample user roster with roles to consider in access rules
- input/resources.json — example posts/resources with owners to consider for ownership checks

Deliverables to create:
1) output/api_spec.json — an implementation-agnostic API security spec in this exact JSON shape:
{
  "tokens": {
    "access_ttl": "15m",
    "refresh_ttl": "7d"
  },
  "rate_limits": {
    "login": { "max": 5, "window": "15m" },
    "api": { "max": 100, "window": "1m" }
  },
  "endpoints": [
    {"path": "/api/auth/register", "method": "POST", "auth": false},
    {"path": "/api/auth/login", "method": "POST", "auth": false},
    {"path": "/api/auth/refresh", "method": "POST", "auth": false},
    {"path": "/api/users", "method": "GET", "auth": true, "required_permissions": ["read:users"]},
    {"path": "/api/users/{id}", "method": "DELETE", "auth": true, "required_permissions": ["delete:users"]},
    {"path": "/api/posts", "method": "POST", "auth": true, "required_permissions": ["write:posts"]},
    {"path": "/api/posts/{id}", "method": "PUT", "auth": true, "required_permissions": ["write:posts"], "ownership": "owner_only"}
  ]
}
- Keep the structure exactly as shown but you may add more endpoints if needed. The required endpoints above must be present with the listed keys and values.

2) output/rbac.json — a role and permission policy file with this structure and content:
{
  "roles": {
    "user": ["read:posts", "write:posts"],
    "moderator": ["read:posts", "write:posts", "read:users"],
    "admin": ["read:posts", "write:posts", "read:users", "write:users", "delete:users"]
  },
  "hierarchy": {
    "admin": ["admin", "moderator", "user"],
    "moderator": ["moderator", "user"],
    "user": ["user"]
  }
}
- You may include additional permissions if you want, but these exact baseline permissions and hierarchy must be present.

3) output/security_design.md — a detailed security design (text) that addresses all of the following:
- Token model and lifetimes: explicitly state access tokens are short-lived at 15 minutes and refresh tokens are 7 days. Describe the refresh flow and server-side revocation on logout and the ability to revoke all refresh tokens for a user.
- Password policy and hashing: explicitly require a minimum of 12 characters with at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character; and state that password hashing uses a cost factor of 12 or more rounds (avoid naming any specific library).
- Session/cookie security (for the session-based variant): explicitly mention httpOnly, secure, and sameSite cookie flags.
- Rate limiting: explicitly document a login rate limit of 5 attempts per 15 minutes and a general API limit of 100 requests per minute.
- Resource ownership enforcement: explain how users can modify only their own posts unless they have a role that overrides ownership.
- Briefly note secure storage of tokens (do not recommend localStorage) and logging of security events.

4) output/test_plan.csv — a comma-separated test plan with headers:
case_id,title,endpoint,method,preconditions,steps,expected_result
Include at least 12 test cases covering: successful login, invalid password, login rate limiting lockout, access with expired access token, successful token refresh, refresh token reuse/revocation handling, RBAC allow and RBAC deny scenarios, permission allow and permission deny scenarios, ownership allow (user updates own post) and ownership deny (user attempts to update another user's post), and admin-only delete user allowed while moderator delete user denied.

Focus on clean, implementation-agnostic security patterns. Do not refer to any specific frameworks or libraries. Write all outputs to the paths above.
